channel.title: The Grant Desk - Research & Journalism, UK, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Research & Journalism, UK, added in last 30 days.

item.title: LSE Research Officer: DigiPulse, Digital Futures for Children Centre
item.description:
Organisation: London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE), Department of Media and Communications

Location: London, UK (hybrid working)

Award: GBP 43,277 to 51,714 per year inclusive of London allowance, with potential to progress to GBP 55,497. Full-time (1.0 FTE), funded until December 2028. Benefits: occupational pension scheme, generous annual leave, hybrid working, training and development. No application fee.

Duration: Full-time fixed-term post, funded until December 2028

Deadline: 22 Jul 2026

DigiPulse is a three-year longitudinal, international research project in LSE's Digital Futures for Children centre investigating the real-time relationships between children's smartphone use and their wellbeing, with data collected in the UK, Brazil and Kenya. The project combines passive smartphone tracking, Ecological Momentary Assessment (EMA) and longitudinal surveys with around 750 children and young people aged 10-17. THE ROLE: the Research Officer plays a central part in project management, development, data collection and analysis, with particular responsibility for developing and refining EMA instruments, contributing to the research literature and hypotheses, supporting participatory and consultative work with children, and conducting statistical analysis and producing findings, working closely with partners in the UK, Brazil and Kenya. REQUIREMENTS: a completed PhD in a relevant discipline (e.g. media and communications, psychology, psychiatry, sociology, education, or computational/data social science); experience developing surveys or questionnaire instruments; experience designing or conducting longitudinal research studies; and experience with EMA, experience-sampling or other intensive longitudinal data collection methods. APPLY via jobs.lse.ac.uk; questions to k.g.ringmar-sylwander@lse.ac.uk. Closing date 22 July 2026 (23:59 UK time); late applications are not accepted.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
